channel.title: Home on Sharad Raj
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Sharad Raj

item.title: Lead AI Engineer
item.description: Promoted to Lead AI Engineer; led the scaling and optimization of AI solutions and backend systems for Generative AI products, building agentic workflows (Agentic Architecture, AI Agents, LangGraph, MCP) with an emphasis on ethical, human-centered AI at scale.

item.title: AI Engineer
item.description:
AI Engineer focused on building and scaling GenAI solutions with emphasis on production-ready systems and MLOps practices.

  • Developed agentic AI systems and RAG pipelines for enterprise applications
  • Implemented CI/CD workflows and containerized deployments
  • Optimized LLM integrations and backend infrastructure

item.title: Software Developer
item.description: Deployed the “Adarsh Champawat Geospatial Dashboard” for Champawat District, Uttarakhand; led Web GIS dashboards integrated with AI/ML (LLMs + RAG) to optimize data processing and user interactions using GIS/Web GIS approaches.

item.title: Blog authoring showcase (math, figures, code, markdown)
item.description: This post is a living reference for everything you can drop into a normal article: front matter, math, the fig shortcode, plain images, highlighted code with line numbers, links, lists, and quotes. Copy the fenced blocks into your own .md files.

item.title: Agentic RAG Workflows: Building Tool-Using Agents
item.description: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has become a cornerstone of modern AI systems, enabling language models to access external knowledge bases and provide more accurate, contextual responses. However, static RAG systems have limitations when dealing with complex queries that require multiple steps or tool interactions.

item.title: MLOps for LLM Services: CI/CD and Deployment Patterns
item.description:
Deploying large language models in production requires more than just containerization. It demands a comprehensive MLOps strategy that addresses model versioning, performance monitoring, and continuous improvement.

The MLOps Pipeline

A robust MLOps pipeline for LLM services includes:

item.title: Efficient Scaling of Agentic AI Systems in Production
item.description:
1. Introduction

The deployment of agentic AI systems at scale presents unique challenges in terms of latency, cost, and reliability. Traditional approaches to scaling monolithic systems often fail when applied to distributed agentic architectures.

item.title: Agentic RAG Pipeline
item.description:
Overview

The Agentic RAG Pipeline is a production-ready system for building tool-using agents with retrieval-augmented generation capabilities. It provides a flexible framework for creating autonomous agents that can reason, retrieve information, and take actions.

item.title: Adarsh Champawat Geospatial Dashboard
item.description:
Overview

The Adarsh Champawat Geospatial Dashboard is a comprehensive Web GIS application designed for district-level spatial analysis and decision support. Developed for Champawat District in Uttarakhand, India, the dashboard integrates advanced geospatial analysis with AI/ML capabilities.
